file.content:
WORKDIR = $(PWD)
GOCMD = go

# Build output
CLI_BIN = $(WORKDIR)/build/bin

# Upstream git (for test infrastructure)
GIT_CLI_VERSION ?= v2.47.0
GIT_DIST_PATH ?= $(WORKDIR)/.git-dist
GIT_REPOSITORY = http://github.com/git/git.git

.PHONY: build build-git-testdeps test-cli test-cli-verbose gen-skip-list classify-failures clean

build:
	mkdir -p $(CLI_BIN)
	$(GOCMD) build -o $(CLI_BIN)/git ./cmd/git/...
	$(CLI_BIN)/git install

# Clone and build upstream git into .git-dist/ (needed for test-lib.sh,
# test-tool helpers, etc.). Cached â€” only clones/builds once.
build-git-testdeps:
	@if [ -f $(GIT_DIST_PATH)/git ]; then \
		echo "using cached git build at $(GIT_DIST_PATH)"; \
	else \
		echo "Cloning git $(GIT_CLI_VERSION) into $(GIT_DIST_PATH)..."; \
		git clone $(GIT_REPOSITORY) -b $(GIT_CLI_VERSION) --depth 1 --single-branch $(GIT_DIST_PATH); \
		echo "Building git in $(GIT_DIST_PATH)..."; \
		cd $(GIT_DIST_PATH) && make -j$$(nproc 2>/dev/null || sysctl -n hw.ncpu 2>/dev/null || echo 4) all; \
	fi

# Run git test file(s) against the go-git CLI.
# Usage:
#   make test-cli                                  # run all key suites
#   make test-cli T=t7004-tag.sh                   # run a specific test
#   make test-cli T="t0001-init.sh t7004-tag.sh"   # run multiple
#   make test-cli-verbose T=t0001-init.sh          # verbose single test
test-cli: build build-git-testdeps
	@bash test/run-tests.sh $(GIT_DIST_PATH) $(CLI_BIN) $(T)

test-cli-verbose: build build-git-testdeps
ifndef T
	$(error T is required. Usage: make test-cli-verbose T=t0001-init.sh)
endif
	@bash test/run-tests.sh $(GIT_DIST_PATH) $(CLI_BIN) --verbose $(T)

# Re-generate the skip list by detecting which failures hit unimplemented commands.
gen-skip-list: build build-git-testdeps
	bash test/gen-skip-list.sh $(GIT_DIST_PATH) $(CLI_BIN)

# Classify test failures into categories (CLI shim vs go-git library bugs).
classify-failures: build build-git-testdeps
	@bash test/classify-failures.sh $(GIT_DIST_PATH) $(CLI_BIN) $(T)

clean:
	rm -rf $(GIT_DIST_PATH) build/

1â†’package main
     2â†’
     3â†’import (
     4â†’	"fmt"
     5â†’	"os"
     6â†’	"path/filepath"
     7â†’	"strings"
     8â†’)
     9â†’
    10â†’// command is a subcommand handler. It receives the remaining args after the
    11â†’// subcommand name and returns an exit code.
    12â†’type command func(args []string) int
    13â†’
    14â†’var commands map[string]command
    15â†’
    16â†’// allCommands returns the list of known subcommand names (for install symlinks).
    17â†’func allCommands() []string {
    18â†’	names := make([]string, 0, len(commands))
    19â†’	for name := range commands {
    20â†’		names = append(names, name)
    21â†’	}
    22â†’	return names
    23â†’}
    24â†’
    25â†’func init() {
    26â†’	commands = map[string]command{
    27â†’		"version":      cmdVersion,
    28â†’		"init":         cmdInit,
    29â†’		"config":       cmdConfig,
    30â†’		"install":      cmdInstall,
    31â†’		"hash-object":  cmdHashObject,
    32â†’		"update-ref":   cmdUpdateRef,
    33â†’		"symbolic-ref": cmdSymbolicRef,
    34â†’		"show-ref":     cmdShowRef,
    35â†’		"rev-parse":    cmdRevParse,
    36â†’		"cat-file":     cmdCatFile,
    37â†’		"ls-tree":      cmdLsTree,
    38â†’		"add":          cmdAdd,
    39â†’		"commit":       cmdCommit,
    40â†’		"status":       cmdStatus,
    41â†’		"branch":       cmdBranch,
    42â†’		"tag":          cmdTag,
    43â†’		"log":          cmdLog,
    44â†’		"checkout":     cmdCheckout,
    45â†’		"remote":       cmdRemote,
    46â†’		"push":         cmdPush,
    47â†’		"fetch":        cmdFetch,
    48â†’		"show":          cmdShow,
    49â†’		"read-tree":     cmdReadTree,
    50â†’		"pack-refs":     cmdPackRefs,
    51â†’		"diff":          cmdUnimplemented,
    52â†’		"diff-files":    cmdUnimplemented,
    53â†’		"diff-index":    cmdUnimplemented,
    54â†’		"merge":         cmdUnimplemented,
    55â†’		"reset":         cmdReset,
    56â†’		"rm":            cmdUnimplemented,
    57â†’		"clone":         cmdClone,
    58â†’		"update-index":  cmdUpdateIndex,
    59â†’		"reflog":        cmdReflog,
    60â†’		"worktree":      cmdWorktree,
    61â†’		"write-tree":    cmdWriteTree,
    62â†’		"commit-tree":   cmdCommitTree,
    63â†’		"ls-files":      cmdLsFiles,
    64â†’		"switch":        cmdSwitch,
    65â†’		"for-each-ref":  cmdForEachRef,
    66â†’		"pull":          cmdUnimplemented,
    67â†’	}
    68â†’}
    69â†’
    70â†’func main() {
    71â†’	// Multicall: if invoked as "git-foo", treat "foo" as the subcommand.
    72â†’	base := filepath.Base(os.Args[0])
    73â†’	if strings.HasPrefix(base, "git-") {
    74â†’		subcmd := strings.TrimPrefix(base, "git-")
    75â†’		os.Exit(dispatch(subcmd, os.Args[1:]))
    76â†’	}
    77â†’
    78â†’	// Normal: "git [global-flags] <command> [args...]"
    79â†’	globals, subcmd, args := parseGlobalFlags(os.Args[1:])
    80â†’
    81â†’	if globals.showVersion {
    82â†’		os.Exit(cmdVersion(nil))
    83â†’	}
    84â†’	if globals.showExecPath {
    85â†’		fmt.Println(execPath())
    86â†’		os.Exit(0)
    87â†’	}
    88â†’
    89â†’	if subcmd == "" {
    90â†’		fmt.Fprintln(os.Stderr, "usage: git [--version] [--exec-path] [-C <path>] [-c <key>=<value>] <command> [<args>]")
    91â†’		os.Exit(1)
    92â†’	}
    93â†’
    94â†’	applyGlobals(globals)
    95â†’	// Prepend any forwarded global flags to the subcommand args.
    96â†’	if len(globals.forwardArgs) > 0 {
    97â†’		args = append(globals.forwardArgs, args...)
    98â†’	}
    99â†’	os.Exit(dispatch(subcmd, args))
   100â†’}
   101â†’
   102â†’func dispatch(name string, args []string) int {
   103â†’	fn, ok := commands[name]
   104â†’	if !ok {
   105â†’		logUnimplemented(name)
   106â†’		fmt.Fprintf(os.Stderr, "git: '%s' is not a git command. See 'git --help'.\n", name)
   107â†’		return 1
   108â†’	}
   109â†’	return fn(args)
   110â†’}
   111â†’
   112â†’func logUnimplemented(cmd string) {
   113â†’	logPath := os.Getenv("GIT_TEST_UNIMPLEMENTED_LOG")
   114â†’	if logPath == "" {
   115â†’		return
   116â†’	}
   117â†’	f, err := os.OpenFile(logPath, os.O_APPEND|os.O_CREATE|os.O_WRONLY, 0o644)
   118â†’	if err != nil {
   119â†’		return
   120â†’	}
   121â†’	defer f.Close()
   122â†’	fmt.Fprintf(f, "%s\n", cmd)
   123â†’}
   124â†’
   125â†’func cmdUnimplemented(args []string) int {
   126â†’	// Recover the command name from argv[0] or args.
   127â†’	cmd := "unknown"
   128â†’	base := filepath.Base(os.Args[0])
   129â†’	if strings.HasPrefix(base, "git-") {
   130â†’		cmd = strings.TrimPrefix(base, "git-")
   131â†’	} else if len(os.Args) > 1 {
   132â†’		cmd = os.Args[1]
   133â†’	}
   134â†’	logUnimplemented(cmd)
   135â†’	fmt.Fprintf(os.Stderr, "fatal: command not yet implemented in go-git\n")
   136â†’	return 128
   137â†’}
   138â†’

input.content:
# git-cli

A drop-in replacement for the `git` command-line interface, built entirely on top of [go-git](https://github.com/go-git/go-git). This is **not** intended as a production Git client â€” its purpose is to validate the go-git library implementation against the official Git CLI by running Git's own test suite against it.

## How it works

The binary implements Git subcommands (init, commit, push, log, etc.) by translating CLI flags and arguments into go-git library calls. It uses a multicall binary pattern â€” a single `git` binary plus `git-<command>` symlinks â€” so it can be swapped into Git's test harness transparently.

### Implemented commands

add, branch, cat-file, checkout, clone, commit, commit-tree, config, fetch, for-each-ref, hash-object, init, log, ls-files, ls-tree, pack-refs, push, read-tree, reflog, remote, reset, rev-parse, show, show-ref, status, switch, symbolic-ref, tag, update-index, update-ref, version, worktree, write-tree

### Not yet implemented

diff, diff-files, diff-index, merge, pull, rm

## Building

```
make build
```

This compiles the binary to `build/bin/git` and runs `git install` to create the symlinks.

## Testing against upstream Git

The test infrastructure clones upstream Git and runs its shell-based test suite with this binary substituted in:

```
# Run all test suites
make test-cli

# Run a specific test file
make test-cli T=t0001-init.sh

# Verbose output for a single test
make test-cli-verbose T=t0001-init.sh
```

Requires an internet connection on first run to clone the upstream Git source (cached in `.git-dist/`).

## Requirements

- Go 1.24+
- A local clone of [go-git](https://github.com/go-git/go-git) at `../go-git` (see `replace` directive in `go.mod`)

content.text: Done. The README covers the project's purpose as a go-git validation tool, lists implemented/unimplemented commands, and documents building and running the upstream Git test suite.

message.content: I'd like to also add that we can easily build it against the real repo (go-git/go-git) but also forks like (Soph/go-git) so people can validate changes against the test suite, how can we achieve that?

content.text: The simplest approach is a Makefile variable that swaps the `replace` directive via `go mod edit` before building. That way users can point at a local path, a fork, or a specific branch/commit.

file.content:
WORKDIR = $(PWD)
GOCMD = go

# Build output
CLI_BIN = $(WORKDIR)/build/bin

# Upstream git (for test infrastructure)
GIT_CLI_VERSION ?= v2.47.0
GIT_DIST_PATH ?= $(WORKDIR)/.git-dist
GIT_REPOSITORY = http://github.com/git/git.git

.PHONY: build build-git-testdeps test-cli test-cli-verbose gen-skip-list classify-failures clean

build:
	mkdir -p $(CLI_BIN)
	$(GOCMD) build -o $(CLI_BIN)/git ./cmd/git/...
	$(CLI_BIN)/git install

# Clone and build upstream git into .git-dist/ (needed for test-lib.sh,
# test-tool helpers, etc.). Cached â€” only clones/builds once.
build-git-testdeps:
	@if [ -f $(GIT_DIST_PATH)/git ]; then \
		echo "using cached git build at $(GIT_DIST_PATH)"; \
	else \
		echo "Cloning git $(GIT_CLI_VERSION) into $(GIT_DIST_PATH)..."; \
		git clone $(GIT_REPOSITORY) -b $(GIT_CLI_VERSION) --depth 1 --single-branch $(GIT_DIST_PATH); \
		echo "Building git in $(GIT_DIST_PATH)..."; \
		cd $(GIT_DIST_PATH) && make -j$$(nproc 2>/dev/null || sysctl -n hw.ncpu 2>/dev/null || echo 4) all; \
	fi

# Run git test file(s) against the go-git CLI.
# Usage:
#   make test-cli                                  # run all key suites
#   make test-cli T=t7004-tag.sh                   # run a specific test
#   make test-cli T="t0001-init.sh t7004-tag.sh"   # run multiple
#   make test-cli-verbose T=t0001-init.sh          # verbose single test
test-cli: build build-git-testdeps
	@bash test/run-tests.sh $(GIT_DIST_PATH) $(CLI_BIN) $(T)

test-cli-verbose: build build-git-testdeps
ifndef T
	$(error T is required. Usage: make test-cli-verbose T=t0001-init.sh)
endif
	@bash test/run-tests.sh $(GIT_DIST_PATH) $(CLI_BIN) --verbose $(T)

# Re-generate the skip list by detecting which failures hit unimplemented commands.
gen-skip-list: build build-git-testdeps
	bash test/gen-skip-list.sh $(GIT_DIST_PATH) $(CLI_BIN)

# Classify test failures into categories (CLI shim vs go-git library bugs).
classify-failures: build build-git-testdeps
	@bash test/classify-failures.sh $(GIT_DIST_PATH) $(CLI_BIN) $(T)

clean:
	rm -rf $(GIT_DIST_PATH) build/

content.text: Here's my proposal: add a `GOGIT` variable to the Makefile that runs `go mod edit -replace` before compiling. It accepts either a local path (default `../go-git`) or a remote module like `github.com/Soph/go-git/v6@branch-name`.

input.new_string:
WORKDIR = $(PWD)
GOCMD = go

# Build output
CLI_BIN = $(WORKDIR)/build/bin

# go-git module to build against. Accepts:
#   Local path (default):  GOGIT=../go-git
#   Fork + branch/tag:     GOGIT=github.com/Soph/go-git/v6@my-branch
#   Fork + commit:         GOGIT=github.com/Soph/go-git/v6@abc1234
GOGIT ?= ../go-git

# Upstream git (for test infrastructure)
GIT_CLI_VERSION ?= v2.47.0
GIT_DIST_PATH ?= $(WORKDIR)/.git-dist
GIT_REPOSITORY = http://github.com/git/git.git

.PHONY: build set-gogit build-git-testdeps test-cli test-cli-verbose gen-skip-list classify-failures clean

# Point the go.mod replace directive at the chosen go-git source.
set-gogit:
	$(GOCMD) mod edit -replace github.com/go-git/go-git/v6=$(GOGIT)
	@# For remote modules, tidy to resolve the version; local paths need no tidy.
	@case "$(GOGIT)" in /*|./*|../*) ;; *) $(GOCMD) mod tidy ;; esac

build: set-gogit
	mkdir -p $(CLI_BIN)
	$(GOCMD) build -o $(CLI_BIN)/git ./cmd/git/...
	$(CLI_BIN)/git install

file.content:
# git-cli

A drop-in replacement for the `git` command-line interface, built entirely on top of [go-git](https://github.com/go-git/go-git). This is **not** intended as a production Git client â€” its purpose is to validate the go-git library implementation against the official Git CLI by running Git's own test suite against it.

## How it works

The binary implements Git subcommands (init, commit, push, log, etc.) by translating CLI flags and arguments into go-git library calls. It uses a multicall binary pattern â€” a single `git` binary plus `git-<command>` symlinks â€” so it can be swapped into Git's test harness transparently.

### Implemented commands

add, branch, cat-file, checkout, clone, commit, commit-tree, config, fetch, for-each-ref, hash-object, init, log, ls-files, ls-tree, pack-refs, push, read-tree, reflog, remote, reset, rev-parse, show, show-ref, status, switch, symbolic-ref, tag, update-index, update-ref, version, worktree, write-tree

### Not yet implemented

diff, diff-files, diff-index, merge, pull, rm

## Building

```
make build
```

This compiles the binary to `build/bin/git` and runs `git install` to create the symlinks.

## Testing against upstream Git

The test infrastructure clones upstream Git and runs its shell-based test suite with this binary substituted in:

```
# Run all test suites
make test-cli

# Run a specific test file
make test-cli T=t0001-init.sh

# Verbose output for a single test
make test-cli-verbose T=t0001-init.sh
```

Requires an internet connection on first run to clone the upstream Git source (cached in `.git-dist/`).

## Requirements

- Go 1.24+
- A local clone of [go-git](https://github.com/go-git/go-git) at `../go-git` (see `replace` directive in `go.mod`)

## Building

By default, the build uses a local clone of go-git at `../go-git`:

```
make build
```

### Building against a fork or branch

Use the `GOGIT` variable to build against any go-git fork, branch, or commit. This makes it easy to validate changes on a fork before they land upstream.

```
# Local path (default)
make build GOGIT=../go-git

# Upstream go-git at a specific tag
make build GOGIT=github.com/go-git/go-git/v6@v6.1.0

# Your fork at a branch
make build GOGIT=github.com/Soph/go-git/v6@my-feature-branch

# Your fork at a specific commit
make build GOGIT=github.com/Soph/go-git/v6@abc1234

# Build + run the test suite against your fork
make test-cli GOGIT=github.com/Soph/go-git/v6@my-feature-branch
```

This updates the `replace` directive in `go.mod` and, for remote modules, runs `go mod tidy` to resolve the version.

## Testing against upstream Git

Here's what changed:

**Makefile** â€” Added a `GOGIT` variable (defaults to `../go-git`) and a `set-gogit` target that runs `go mod edit -replace` before every build. For remote modules (anything that isn't a relative/absolute path) it also runs `go mod tidy` to resolve the version.

**README** â€” Documents the `GOGIT` variable with examples for local paths, upstream tags, fork branches, and specific commits.

Usage is straightforward:
```
# Validate your fork's branch against Git's test suite
make test-cli GOGIT=github.com/Soph/go-git/v6@my-feature-branch
```

file.content:
WORKDIR = $(PWD)
GOCMD = go

# Build output
CLI_BIN = $(WORKDIR)/build/bin

# go-git module to build against. Accepts:
#   Local path (default):  GOGIT=../go-git
#   Fork + branch/tag:     GOGIT=github.com/Soph/go-git/v6@my-branch
#   Fork + commit:         GOGIT=github.com/Soph/go-git/v6@abc1234
GOGIT ?= ../go-git

# Upstream git (for test infrastructure)
GIT_CLI_VERSION ?= v2.47.0
GIT_DIST_PATH ?= $(WORKDIR)/.git-dist
GIT_REPOSITORY = http://github.com/git/git.git

.PHONY: build set-gogit build-git-testdeps test-cli test-cli-verbose gen-skip-list classify-failures clean

# Point the go.mod replace directive at the chosen go-git source.
set-gogit:
	$(GOCMD) mod edit -replace github.com/go-git/go-git/v6=$(GOGIT)
	@# For remote modules, tidy to resolve the version; local paths need no tidy.
	@case "$(GOGIT)" in /*|./*|../*) ;; *) $(GOCMD) mod tidy ;; esac

build: set-gogit
	mkdir -p $(CLI_BIN)
	$(GOCMD) build -o $(CLI_BIN)/git ./cmd/git/...
	$(CLI_BIN)/git install

# Clone and build upstream git into .git-dist/ (needed for test-lib.sh,
# test-tool helpers, etc.). Cached â€” only clones/builds once.
build-git-testdeps:
	@if [ -f $(GIT_DIST_PATH)/git ]; then \
		echo "using cached git build at $(GIT_DIST_PATH)"; \
	else \
		echo "Cloning git $(GIT_CLI_VERSION) into $(GIT_DIST_PATH)..."; \
		git clone $(GIT_REPOSITORY) -b $(GIT_CLI_VERSION) --depth 1 --single-branch $(GIT_DIST_PATH); \
		echo "Building git in $(GIT_DIST_PATH)..."; \
		cd $(GIT_DIST_PATH) && make -j$$(nproc 2>/dev/null || sysctl -n hw.ncpu 2>/dev/null || echo 4) all; \
	fi

# Run git test file(s) against the go-git CLI.
# Usage:
#   make test-cli                                  # run all key suites
#   make test-cli T=t7004-tag.sh                   # run a specific test
#   make test-cli T="t0001-init.sh t7004-tag.sh"   # run multiple
#   make test-cli-verbose T=t0001-init.sh          # verbose single test
test-cli: build build-git-testdeps
	@bash test/run-tests.sh $(GIT_DIST_PATH) $(CLI_BIN) $(T)

test-cli-verbose: build build-git-testdeps
ifndef T
	$(error T is required. Usage: make test-cli-verbose T=t0001-init.sh)
endif
	@bash test/run-tests.sh $(GIT_DIST_PATH) $(CLI_BIN) --verbose $(T)

# Re-generate the skip list by detecting which failures hit unimplemented commands.
gen-skip-list: build build-git-testdeps
	bash test/gen-skip-list.sh $(GIT_DIST_PATH) $(CLI_BIN)

# Classify test failures into categories (CLI shim vs go-git library bugs).
classify-failures: build build-git-testdeps
	@bash test/classify-failures.sh $(GIT_DIST_PATH) $(CLI_BIN) $(T)

clean:
	rm -rf $(GIT_DIST_PATH) build/

input.old_string:
# Point the go.mod replace directive at the chosen go-git source.
set-gogit:
	$(GOCMD) mod edit -replace github.com/go-git/go-git/v6=$(GOGIT)
	@# For remote modules, tidy to resolve the version; local paths need no tidy.
	@case "$(GOGIT)" in /*|./*|../*) ;; *) $(GOCMD) mod tidy ;; esac
input.new_string:
# Point the go.mod replace directive at the chosen go-git source.
# For local paths, go mod edit works directly. For remote modules (e.g.
# github.com/Soph/go-git/v6@branch-name), we first resolve the branch/tag
# to a pseudo-version via "go list -m", because go mod edit only accepts
# semver versions.
set-gogit:
	@GOGIT="$(GOGIT)"; \
	case "$$GOGIT" in \
		/*|./*|../*) \
			$(GOCMD) mod edit -replace github.com/go-git/go-git/v6=$$GOGIT ;; \
		*@*) \
			MOD="$${GOGIT%%@*}"; REF="$${GOGIT#*@}"; \
			echo "resolving $$MOD@$$REF ..."; \
			VERSION=$$(GOPROXY=direct $(GOCMD) list -m -f '{{.Version}}' "$$MOD@$$REF") || exit 1; \
			echo "resolved to $$VERSION"; \
			$(GOCMD) mod edit -replace "github.com/go-git/go-git/v6=$$MOD@$$VERSION"; \
			$(GOCMD) mod tidy ;; \
		*) \
			$(GOCMD) mod edit -replace github.com/go-git/go-git/v6=$$GOGIT; \
			$(GOCMD) mod tidy ;; \
	esac

content.text:
The fix: for remote modules with `@branch-name`, it now uses `go list -m` (with `GOPROXY=direct` so it hits GitHub directly) to resolve the branch name to a proper pseudo-version, then passes that to `go mod edit -replace`.

Try it again:
```
make test-cli GOGIT=github.com/Soph/go-git/v6@handle-config-values-more-inline-with-git-cli
```

You should see it print something like `resolved to v6.0.0-20260320...` before building.

toolUseResult.originalFile:
WORKDIR = $(PWD)
GOCMD = go

# Build output
CLI_BIN = $(WORKDIR)/build/bin

# go-git module to build against. Accepts:
#   Local path (default):  GOGIT=../go-git
#   Fork + branch/tag:     GOGIT=github.com/Soph/go-git/v6@my-branch
#   Fork + commit:         GOGIT=github.com/Soph/go-git/v6@abc1234
GOGIT ?= ../go-git

# Upstream git (for test infrastructure)
GIT_CLI_VERSION ?= v2.47.0
GIT_DIST_PATH ?= $(WORKDIR)/.git-dist
GIT_REPOSITORY = http://github.com/git/git.git

.PHONY: build set-gogit build-git-testdeps test-cli test-cli-verbose gen-skip-list classify-failures clean

# Point the go.mod replace directive at the chosen go-git source.
# For local paths, go mod edit works directly. For remote modules (e.g.
# github.com/Soph/go-git/v6@branch-name), we first resolve the branch/tag
# to a pseudo-version via "go list -m", because go mod edit only accepts
# semver versions.
set-gogit:
	@GOGIT="$(GOGIT)"; \
	case "$$GOGIT" in \
		/*|./*|../*) \
			$(GOCMD) mod edit -replace github.com/go-git/go-git/v6=$$GOGIT ;; \
		*@*) \
			MOD="$${GOGIT%%@*}"; REF="$${GOGIT#*@}"; \
			echo "resolving $$MOD@$$REF ..."; \
			VERSION=$$(GOPROXY=direct $(GOCMD) list -m -f '{{.Version}}' "$$MOD@$$REF") || exit 1; \
			echo "resolved to $$VERSION"; \
			$(GOCMD) mod edit -replace "github.com/go-git/go-git/v6=$$MOD@$$VERSION"; \
			$(GOCMD) mod tidy ;; \
		*) \
			$(GOCMD) mod edit -replace github.com/go-git/go-git/v6=$$GOGIT; \
			$(GOCMD) mod tidy ;; \
	esac

build: set-gogit
	mkdir -p $(CLI_BIN)
	$(GOCMD) build -o $(CLI_BIN)/git ./cmd/git/...
	$(CLI_BIN)/git install

# Clone and build upstream git into .git-dist/ (needed for test-lib.sh,
# test-tool helpers, etc.). Cached â€” only clones/builds once.
build-git-testdeps:
	@if [ -f $(GIT_DIST_PATH)/git ]; then \
		echo "using cached git build at $(GIT_DIST_PATH)"; \
	else \
		echo "Cloning git $(GIT_CLI_VERSION) into $(GIT_DIST_PATH)..."; \
		git clone $(GIT_REPOSITORY) -b $(GIT_CLI_VERSION) --depth 1 --single-branch $(GIT_DIST_PATH); \
		echo "Building git in $(GIT_DIST_PATH)..."; \
		cd $(GIT_DIST_PATH) && make -j$$(nproc 2>/dev/null || sysctl -n hw.ncpu 2>/dev/null || echo 4) all; \
	fi

# Run git test file(s) against the go-git CLI.
# Usage:
#   make test-cli                                  # run all key suites
#   make test-cli T=t7004-tag.sh                   # run a specific test
#   make test-cli T="t0001-init.sh t7004-tag.sh"   # run multiple
#   make test-cli-verbose T=t0001-init.sh          # verbose single test
test-cli: build build-git-testdeps
	@bash test/run-tests.sh $(GIT_DIST_PATH) $(CLI_BIN) $(T)

test-cli-verbose: build build-git-testdeps
ifndef T
	$(error T is required. Usage: make test-cli-verbose T=t0001-init.sh)
endif
	@bash test/run-tests.sh $(GIT_DIST_PATH) $(CLI_BIN) --verbose $(T)

# Re-generate the skip list by detecting which failures hit unimplemented commands.
gen-skip-list: build build-git-testdeps
	bash test/gen-skip-list.sh $(GIT_DIST_PATH) $(CLI_BIN)

# Classify test failures into categories (CLI shim vs go-git library bugs).
classify-failures: build build-git-testdeps
	@bash test/classify-failures.sh $(GIT_DIST_PATH) $(CLI_BIN) $(T)

clean:
	rm -rf $(GIT_DIST_PATH) build/

content.text:
The key change: `go mod edit -dropreplace` now runs first, clearing any existing replace directive so `go.mod` is always in a parseable state before `go list -m` tries to resolve the branch name. Try it again:

```
make test-cli GOGIT=github.com/Soph/go-git/v6@handle-config-values-more-inline-with-git-cli
```

message.content:
â‌¯ make test-cli GOGIT=github.com/Soph/go-git/v6@handle-config-values-more-inline-with-git-cli
resolving github.com/Soph/go-git/v6@handle-config-values-more-inline-with-git-cli ...
go: github.com/Soph/go-git/v6@handle-config-values-more-inline-with-git-cli: invalid version: unknown revision handle-config-values-more-inline-with-git-cli

content.text:
The issue is that after `dropreplace`, `go.mod` has a `require` with a dummy version (`v6.0.0-00010101000000-000000000000`) and no replace â€” so any `go` command fails trying to parse it before it can resolve anything.

The fix: run `go list -m` from `/tmp` so it doesn't see the project's broken `go.mod`.

file.content:
WORKDIR = $(PWD)
GOCMD = go

# Build output
CLI_BIN = $(WORKDIR)/build/bin

# go-git module to build against. Accepts:
#   Local path (default):  GOGIT=../go-git
#   Fork + branch/tag:     GOGIT=github.com/Soph/go-git/v6@my-branch
#   Fork + commit:         GOGIT=github.com/Soph/go-git/v6@abc1234
GOGIT ?= ../go-git

# Upstream git (for test infrastructure)
GIT_CLI_VERSION ?= v2.47.0
GIT_DIST_PATH ?= $(WORKDIR)/.git-dist
GIT_REPOSITORY = http://github.com/git/git.git

.PHONY: build set-gogit build-git-testdeps test-cli test-cli-verbose gen-skip-list classify-failures clean

# Point the go.mod replace directive at the chosen go-git source.
# For local paths, go mod edit works directly. For remote modules (e.g.
# github.com/Soph/go-git/v6@branch-name), we first resolve the branch/tag
# to a pseudo-version via "go list -m", because go mod edit only accepts
# semver versions.
set-gogit:
	@GOGIT="$(GOGIT)"; \
	$(GOCMD) mod edit -dropreplace github.com/go-git/go-git/v6; \
	case "$$GOGIT" in \
		/*|./*|../*) \
			$(GOCMD) mod edit -replace github.com/go-git/go-git/v6=$$GOGIT ;; \
		*@*) \
			MOD="$${GOGIT%%@*}"; REF="$${GOGIT#*@}"; \
			echo "resolving $$MOD@$$REF ..."; \
			VERSION=$$(GOPROXY=direct $(GOCMD) list -m -f '{{.Version}}' "$$MOD@$$REF") || exit 1; \
			echo "resolved to $$VERSION"; \
			$(GOCMD) mod edit -replace "github.com/go-git/go-git/v6=$$MOD@$$VERSION"; \
			$(GOCMD) mod tidy ;; \
		*) \
			$(GOCMD) mod edit -replace github.com/go-git/go-git/v6=$$GOGIT; \
			$(GOCMD) mod tidy ;; \
	esac

build: set-gogit
	mkdir -p $(CLI_BIN)
	$(GOCMD) build -o $(CLI_BIN)/git ./cmd/git/...
	$(CLI_BIN)/git install

# Clone and build upstream git into .git-dist/ (needed for test-lib.sh,
# test-tool helpers, etc.). Cached â€” only clones/builds once.
build-git-testdeps:
	@if [ -f $(GIT_DIST_PATH)/git ]; then \
		echo "using cached git build at $(GIT_DIST_PATH)"; \
	else \
		echo "Cloning git $(GIT_CLI_VERSION) into $(GIT_DIST_PATH)..."; \
		git clone $(GIT_REPOSITORY) -b $(GIT_CLI_VERSION) --depth 1 --single-branch $(GIT_DIST_PATH); \
		echo "Building git in $(GIT_DIST_PATH)..."; \
		cd $(GIT_DIST_PATH) && make -j$$(nproc 2>/dev/null || sysctl -n hw.ncpu 2>/dev/null || echo 4) all; \
	fi

# Run git test file(s) against the go-git CLI.
# Usage:
#   make test-cli                                  # run all key suites
#   make test-cli T=t7004-tag.sh                   # run a specific test
#   make test-cli T="t0001-init.sh t7004-tag.sh"   # run multiple
#   make test-cli-verbose T=t0001-init.sh          # verbose single test
test-cli: build build-git-testdeps
	@bash test/run-tests.sh $(GIT_DIST_PATH) $(CLI_BIN) $(T)

test-cli-verbose: build build-git-testdeps
ifndef T
	$(error T is required. Usage: make test-cli-verbose T=t0001-init.sh)
endif
	@bash test/run-tests.sh $(GIT_DIST_PATH) $(CLI_BIN) --verbose $(T)

# Re-generate the skip list by detecting which failures hit unimplemented commands.
gen-skip-list: build build-git-testdeps
	bash test/gen-skip-list.sh $(GIT_DIST_PATH) $(CLI_BIN)

# Classify test failures into categories (CLI shim vs go-git library bugs).
classify-failures: build build-git-testdeps
	@bash test/classify-failures.sh $(GIT_DIST_PATH) $(CLI_BIN) $(T)

clean:
	rm -rf $(GIT_DIST_PATH) build/

			MOD="$${GOGIT%%@*}"; REF="$${GOGIT#*@}"; \

			$(GOCMD) mod edit -replace "github.com/go-git/go-git/v6=$$MOD@$$VERSION"; \
			$(GOCMD) mod tidy ;; \

content.text:
The fix: `go list -m` now runs from `/tmp` with `GOWORK=off`, so it won't see or try to parse the project's `go.mod` (which is in a broken state after `dropreplace`). This lets the version query resolve cleanly against the Go module proxy.

Try again:
```
make test-cli GOGIT=github.com/Soph/go-git/v6@handle-config-values-more-inline-with-git-cli
```

resolving github.com/Soph/go-git/v6@handle-config-values-more-inline-with-git-cli ...
go: github.com/Soph/go-git/v6@handle-config-values-more-inline-with-git-cli: invalid version: unknown revision handle-config-values-more-inline-with-git-cli
make: *** [set-gogit] Error 1

content.text:
The root cause is that `go list -m github.com/Soph/go-git/v6@branch` can never work â€” the fork's `go.mod` declares `module github.com/go-git/go-git/v6`, not `github.com/Soph/go-git/v6`, so Go's module resolution rejects it regardless.

The reliable approach: shallow-clone the fork locally and use a local path replace (which skips module path validation). We can cache it in `.gogit-src/` so repeated builds are fast.

file.content:
WORKDIR = $(PWD)
GOCMD = go

# Build output
CLI_BIN = $(WORKDIR)/build/bin

# go-git module to build against. Accepts:
#   Local path (default):  GOGIT=../go-git
#   Fork + branch/tag:     GOGIT=github.com/Soph/go-git/v6@my-branch
#   Fork + commit:         GOGIT=github.com/Soph/go-git/v6@abc1234
GOGIT ?= ../go-git

# Upstream git (for test infrastructure)
GIT_CLI_VERSION ?= v2.47.0
GIT_DIST_PATH ?= $(WORKDIR)/.git-dist
GIT_REPOSITORY = http://github.com/git/git.git

.PHONY: build set-gogit build-git-testdeps test-cli test-cli-verbose gen-skip-list classify-failures clean

# Point the go.mod replace directive at the chosen go-git source.
# For local paths, go mod edit works directly. For remote modules (e.g.
# github.com/Soph/go-git/v6@branch-name), we first resolve the branch/tag
# to a pseudo-version via "go list -m", because go mod edit only accepts
# semver versions.
set-gogit:
	@GOGIT="$(GOGIT)"; \
	$(GOCMD) mod edit -dropreplace github.com/go-git/go-git/v6; \
	case "$$GOGIT" in \
		/*|./*|../*) \
			$(GOCMD) mod edit -replace github.com/go-git/go-git/v6=$$GOGIT ;; \
		*@*) \
			MOD="$${GOGIT%%@*}"; REF="$${GOGIT#*@}"; \
			echo "resolving $$MOD@$$REF ..."; \
			VERSION=$$(cd /tmp && GOWORK=off GOPROXY=direct $(GOCMD) list -m -f '{{.Version}}' "$$MOD@$$REF") || exit 1; \
			echo "resolved to $$VERSION"; \
			$(GOCMD) mod edit -replace "github.com/go-git/go-git/v6=$$MOD@$$VERSION"; \
			$(GOCMD) mod tidy ;; \
		*) \
			$(GOCMD) mod edit -replace github.com/go-git/go-git/v6=$$GOGIT; \
			$(GOCMD) mod tidy ;; \
	esac

build: set-gogit
	mkdir -p $(CLI_BIN)
	$(GOCMD) build -o $(CLI_BIN)/git ./cmd/git/...
	$(CLI_BIN)/git install

# Clone and build upstream git into .git-dist/ (needed for test-lib.sh,
# test-tool helpers, etc.). Cached â€” only clones/builds once.
build-git-testdeps:
	@if [ -f $(GIT_DIST_PATH)/git ]; then \
		echo "using cached git build at $(GIT_DIST_PATH)"; \
	else \
		echo "Cloning git $(GIT_CLI_VERSION) into $(GIT_DIST_PATH)..."; \
		git clone $(GIT_REPOSITORY) -b $(GIT_CLI_VERSION) --depth 1 --single-branch $(GIT_DIST_PATH); \
		echo "Building git in $(GIT_DIST_PATH)..."; \
		cd $(GIT_DIST_PATH) && make -j$$(nproc 2>/dev/null || sysctl -n hw.ncpu 2>/dev/null || echo 4) all; \
	fi

# Run git test file(s) against the go-git CLI.
# Usage:
#   make test-cli                                  # run all key suites
#   make test-cli T=t7004-tag.sh                   # run a specific test
#   make test-cli T="t0001-init.sh t7004-tag.sh"   # run multiple
#   make test-cli-verbose T=t0001-init.sh          # verbose single test
test-cli: build build-git-testdeps
	@bash test/run-tests.sh $(GIT_DIST_PATH) $(CLI_BIN) $(T)

test-cli-verbose: build build-git-testdeps
ifndef T
	$(error T is required. Usage: make test-cli-verbose T=t0001-init.sh)
endif
	@bash test/run-tests.sh $(GIT_DIST_PATH) $(CLI_BIN) --verbose $(T)

# Re-generate the skip list by detecting which failures hit unimplemented commands.
gen-skip-list: build build-git-testdeps
	bash test/gen-skip-list.sh $(GIT_DIST_PATH) $(CLI_BIN)

# Classify test failures into categories (CLI shim vs go-git library bugs).
classify-failures: build build-git-testdeps
	@bash test/classify-failures.sh $(GIT_DIST_PATH) $(CLI_BIN) $(T)

clean:
	rm -rf $(GIT_DIST_PATH) build/

# Local cache for remote go-git clones.
GOGIT_SRC = $(WORKDIR)/.gogit-src

# Point the go.mod replace directive at the chosen go-git source.

# github.com/Soph/go-git/v6@branch-name), we first resolve the branch/tag

			$(GOCMD) mod edit -replace github.com/go-git/go-git/v6=$$GOGIT ;; \
		*@*) \
			MOD="$${GOGIT%%@*}"; REF="$${GOGIT#*@}"; \
			echo "resolving $$MOD@$$REF ..."; \

input.new_string:
# Point the go.mod replace directive at the chosen go-git source.
# Local paths are used directly. Remote forks (user/repo@ref) are
# shallow-cloned into .gogit-src/ and used as a local replacement,
# because Go module tools cannot resolve forks whose go.mod declares
# a different module path than the fetch URL.
set-gogit:
	@GOGIT="$(GOGIT)"; \
	case "$$GOGIT" in \
		/*|./*|../*) \
			$(GOCMD) mod edit -replace github.com/go-git/go-git/v6=$$GOGIT ;; \
		*@*) \
			REPO_URL="https://$${GOGIT%%@*}"; REF="$${GOGIT#*@}"; \
			if [ -d "$(GOGIT_SRC)/.git" ] && [ "$$(git -C "$(GOGIT_SRC)" remote get-url origin 2>/dev/null)" = "$$REPO_URL" ]; then \
				echo "fetching $$REPO_URL $$REF ..."; \
				git -C "$(GOGIT_SRC)" fetch origin "$$REF"; \
			else \
				rm -rf "$(GOGIT_SRC)"; \
				echo "cloning $$REPO_URL (ref $$REF) ..."; \
				git clone "$$REPO_URL" "$(GOGIT_SRC)"; \
			fi; \
			git -C "$(GOGIT_SRC)" checkout FETCH_HEAD 2>/dev/null \
				|| git -C "$(GOGIT_SRC)" checkout "origin/$$REF" 2>/dev/null \
				|| git -C "$(GOGIT_SRC)" checkout "$$REF"; \
			$(GOCMD) mod edit -replace github.com/go-git/go-git/v6=$(GOGIT_SRC) ;; \
		*) \
			echo "error: GOGIT must be a local path or repo@ref (e.g. github.com/Soph/go-git@branch)"; \
			exit 1 ;; \
	esac

			$(GOCMD) mod edit -replace github.com/go-git/go-git/v6=$$GOGIT ;; \
		*@*) \
			MOD="$${GOGIT%%@*}"; REF="$${GOGIT#*@}"; \
			echo "resolving $$MOD@$$REF ..."; \
			VERSION=$$(cd /tmp && GOWORK=off GOPROXY=direct $(GOCMD) list -m -f '{{.Version}}' "$$MOD@$$REF") || exit 1; \
			echo "resolved to $$VERSION"; \
			$(GOCMD) mod edit -replace "github.com/go-git/go-git/v6=$$MOD@$$VERSION"; \
			$(GOCMD) mod tidy ;; \
		*) \
			$(GOCMD) mod edit -replace github.com/go-git/go-git/v6=$$GOGIT; \
			$(GOCMD) mod tidy ;; \
	esac
toolUseResult.newString:
# Point the go.mod replace directive at the chosen go-git source.
# Local paths are used directly. Remote forks (user/repo@ref) are
# shallow-cloned into .gogit-src/ and used as a local replacement,
# because Go module tools cannot resolve forks whose go.mod declares
# a different module path than the fetch URL.
set-gogit:
	@GOGIT="$(GOGIT)"; \
	case "$$GOGIT" in \
		/*|./*|../*) \
			$(GOCMD) mod edit -replace github.com/go-git/go-git/v6=$$GOGIT ;; \
		*@*) \
			REPO_URL="https://$${GOGIT%%@*}"; REF="$${GOGIT#*@}"; \
			if [ -d "$(GOGIT_SRC)/.git" ] && [ "$$(git -C "$(GOGIT_SRC)" remote get-url origin 2>/dev/null)" = "$$REPO_URL" ]; then \
				echo "fetching $$REPO_URL $$REF ..."; \
				git -C "$(GOGIT_SRC)" fetch origin "$$REF"; \
			else \
				rm -rf "$(GOGIT_SRC)"; \
				echo "cloning $$REPO_URL (ref $$REF) ..."; \
				git clone "$$REPO_URL" "$(GOGIT_SRC)"; \
			fi; \
			git -C "$(GOGIT_SRC)" checkout FETCH_HEAD 2>/dev/null \
				|| git -C "$(GOGIT_SRC)" checkout "origin/$$REF" 2>/dev/null \
				|| git -C "$(GOGIT_SRC)" checkout "$$REF"; \
			$(GOCMD) mod edit -replace github.com/go-git/go-git/v6=$(GOGIT_SRC) ;; \
		*) \
			echo "error: GOGIT must be a local path or repo@ref (e.g. github.com/Soph/go-git@branch)"; \
			exit 1 ;; \
	esac

WORKDIR = $(PWD)
GOCMD = go

# Build output
CLI_BIN = $(WORKDIR)/build/bin

# go-git module to build against. Accepts:
#   Local path (default):  GOGIT=../go-git
#   Remote fork + ref:     GOGIT=github.com/Soph/go-git@my-branch
GOGIT ?= ../go-git

# Local cache for remote go-git clones.
GOGIT_SRC = $(WORKDIR)/.gogit-src

# Upstream git (for test infrastructure)
GIT_CLI_VERSION ?= v2.47.0
GIT_DIST_PATH ?= $(WORKDIR)/.git-dist
GIT_REPOSITORY = http://github.com/git/git.git

.PHONY: build set-gogit build-git-testdeps test-cli test-cli-verbose gen-skip-list classify-failures clean

# Point the go.mod replace directive at the chosen go-git source.
# For local paths, go mod edit works directly. For remote modules (e.g.
# github.com/Soph/go-git/v6@branch-name), we first resolve the branch/tag
# to a pseudo-version via "go list -m", because go mod edit only accepts
# semver versions.
set-gogit:
	@GOGIT="$(GOGIT)"; \
	$(GOCMD) mod edit -dropreplace github.com/go-git/go-git/v6; \
	case "$$GOGIT" in \
		/*|./*|../*) \
			$(GOCMD) mod edit -replace github.com/go-git/go-git/v6=$$GOGIT ;; \
		*@*) \
			MOD="$${GOGIT%%@*}"; REF="$${GOGIT#*@}"; \
			echo "resolving $$MOD@$$REF ..."; \
			VERSION=$$(cd /tmp && GOWORK=off GOPROXY=direct $(GOCMD) list -m -f '{{.Version}}' "$$MOD@$$REF") || exit 1; \
			echo "resolved to $$VERSION"; \
			$(GOCMD) mod edit -replace "github.com/go-git/go-git/v6=$$MOD@$$VERSION"; \
			$(GOCMD) mod tidy ;; \
		*) \
			$(GOCMD) mod edit -replace github.com/go-git/go-git/v6=$$GOGIT; \
			$(GOCMD) mod tidy ;; \
	esac

build: set-gogit
	mkdir -p $(CLI_BIN)
	$(GOCMD) build -o $(CLI_BIN)/git ./cmd/git/...
	$(CLI_BIN)/git install

# Clone and build upstream git into .git-dist/ (needed for test-lib.sh,
# test-tool helpers, etc.). Cached â€” only clones/builds once.
build-git-testdeps:
	@if [ -f $(GIT_DIST_PATH)/git ]; then \
		echo "using cached git build at $(GIT_DIST_PATH)"; \
	else \
		echo "Cloning git $(GIT_CLI_VERSION) into $(GIT_DIST_PATH)..."; \
		git clone $(GIT_REPOSITORY) -b $(GIT_CLI_VERSION) --depth 1 --single-branch $(GIT_DIST_PATH); \
		echo "Building git in $(GIT_DIST_PATH)..."; \
		cd $(GIT_DIST_PATH) && make -j$$(nproc 2>/dev/null || sysctl -n hw.ncpu 2>/dev/null || echo 4) all; \
	fi

# Run git test file(s) against the go-git CLI.
# Usage:
#   make test-cli                                  # run all key suites
#   make test-cli T=t7004-tag.sh                   # run a specific test
#   make test-cli T="t0001-init.sh t7004-tag.sh"   # run multiple
#   make test-cli-verbose T=t0001-init.sh          # verbose single test
test-cli: build build-git-testdeps
	@bash test/run-tests.sh $(GIT_DIST_PATH) $(CLI_BIN) $(T)

test-cli-verbose: build build-git-testdeps
ifndef T
	$(error T is required. Usage: make test-cli-verbose T=t0001-init.sh)
endif
	@bash test/run-tests.sh $(GIT_DIST_PATH) $(CLI_BIN) --verbose $(T)

# Re-generate the skip list by detecting which failures hit unimplemented commands.
gen-skip-list: build build-git-testdeps
	bash test/gen-skip-list.sh $(GIT_DIST_PATH) $(CLI_BIN)

# Classify test failures into categories (CLI shim vs go-git library bugs).
classify-failures: build build-git-testdeps
	@bash test/classify-failures.sh $(GIT_DIST_PATH) $(CLI_BIN) $(T)

clean:
	rm -rf $(GIT_DIST_PATH) build/

file.content:
# git-cli

A drop-in replacement for the `git` command-line interface, built entirely on top of [go-git](https://github.com/go-git/go-git). This is **not** intended as a production Git client â€” its purpose is to validate the go-git library implementation against the official Git CLI by running Git's own test suite against it.

## How it works

The binary implements Git subcommands (init, commit, push, log, etc.) by translating CLI flags and arguments into go-git library calls. It uses a multicall binary pattern â€” a single `git` binary plus `git-<command>` symlinks â€” so it can be swapped into Git's test harness transparently.

### Implemented commands

add, branch, cat-file, checkout, clone, commit, commit-tree, config, fetch, for-each-ref, hash-object, init, log, ls-files, ls-tree, pack-refs, push, read-tree, reflog, remote, reset, rev-parse, show, show-ref, status, switch, symbolic-ref, tag, update-index, update-ref, version, worktree, write-tree

### Not yet implemented

diff, diff-files, diff-index, merge, pull, rm

## Building

By default, the build uses a local clone of go-git at `../go-git`:

```
make build
```

### Building against a fork or branch

Use the `GOGIT` variable to build against any go-git fork, branch, or commit. This makes it easy to validate changes on a fork before they land upstream.

```
# Local path (default)
make build GOGIT=../go-git

# Upstream go-git at a specific tag
make build GOGIT=github.com/go-git/go-git/v6@v6.1.0

# Your fork at a branch
make build GOGIT=github.com/Soph/go-git/v6@my-feature-branch

# Your fork at a specific commit
make build GOGIT=github.com/Soph/go-git/v6@abc1234

# Build + run the test suite against your fork
make test-cli GOGIT=github.com/Soph/go-git/v6@my-feature-branch
```

This updates the `replace` directive in `go.mod` and, for remote modules, runs `go mod tidy` to resolve the version.

## Testing against upstream Git

The test infrastructure clones upstream Git and runs its shell-based test suite with this binary substituted in:

```
# Run all test suites
make test-cli

# Run a specific test file
make test-cli T=t0001-init.sh

# Verbose output for a single test
make test-cli-verbose T=t0001-init.sh
```

Requires an internet connection on first run to clone the upstream Git source (cached in `.git-dist/`).

## Requirements

- Go 1.24+
- A local clone of go-git at `../go-git`, **or** use `GOGIT=` to point at a remote fork/branch

### Building against a fork or branch

Use the `GOGIT` variable to build against any go-git fork or branch. This makes it easy to validate changes on a fork before they land upstream.

```
# Local path (default)
make build GOGIT=../go-git

```
# Local path (default)
make build GOGIT=../go-git

# Upstream go-git at a specific tag
make build GOGIT=github.com/go-git/go-git/v6@v6.1.0

# Your fork at a branch
make build GOGIT=github.com/Soph/go-git/v6@my-feature-branch

# Your fork at a specific commit
make build GOGIT=github.com/Soph/go-git/v6@abc1234

# Build + run the test suite against your fork
make test-cli GOGIT=github.com/Soph/go-git/v6@my-feature-branch
```

This updates the `replace` directive in `go.mod` and, for remote modules, runs `go mod tidy` to resolve the version.

toolUseResult.originalFile:
# git-cli

A drop-in replacement for the `git` command-line interface, built entirely on top of [go-git](https://github.com/go-git/go-git). This is **not** intended as a production Git client â€” its purpose is to validate the go-git library implementation against the official Git CLI by running Git's own test suite against it.

## How it works

The binary implements Git subcommands (init, commit, push, log, etc.) by translating CLI flags and arguments into go-git library calls. It uses a multicall binary pattern â€” a single `git` binary plus `git-<command>` symlinks â€” so it can be swapped into Git's test harness transparently.

### Implemented commands

add, branch, cat-file, checkout, clone, commit, commit-tree, config, fetch, for-each-ref, hash-object, init, log, ls-files, ls-tree, pack-refs, push, read-tree, reflog, remote, reset, rev-parse, show, show-ref, status, switch, symbolic-ref, tag, update-index, update-ref, version, worktree, write-tree

### Not yet implemented

diff, diff-files, diff-index, merge, pull, rm

## Building

By default, the build uses a local clone of go-git at `../go-git`:

```
make build
```

### Building against a fork or branch

Use the `GOGIT` variable to build against any go-git fork, branch, or commit. This makes it easy to validate changes on a fork before they land upstream.

```
# Local path (default)
make build GOGIT=../go-git

# Upstream go-git at a specific tag
make build GOGIT=github.com/go-git/go-git/v6@v6.1.0

# Your fork at a branch
make build GOGIT=github.com/Soph/go-git/v6@my-feature-branch

# Your fork at a specific commit
make build GOGIT=github.com/Soph/go-git/v6@abc1234

# Build + run the test suite against your fork
make test-cli GOGIT=github.com/Soph/go-git/v6@my-feature-branch
```

This updates the `replace` directive in `go.mod` and, for remote modules, runs `go mod tidy` to resolve the version.

## Testing against upstream Git

The test infrastructure clones upstream Git and runs its shell-based test suite with this binary substituted in:

```
# Run all test suites
make test-cli

# Run a specific test file
make test-cli T=t0001-init.sh

# Verbose output for a single test
make test-cli-verbose T=t0001-init.sh
```

Requires an internet connection on first run to clone the upstream Git source (cached in `.git-dist/`).

## Requirements

- Go 1.24+
- A local clone of go-git at `../go-git`, **or** use `GOGIT=` to point at a remote fork/branch

content.text:
The approach is completely different now. Instead of fighting Go's module system (which can't resolve forks whose `go.mod` declares a different module path), remote forks are shallow-cloned into `.gogit-src/` and used as a local path replacement. The clone is cached between runs â€” subsequent builds just `fetch` the latest.

The syntax is also simpler now â€” no `/v6` suffix needed:
```
make test-cli GOGIT=github.com/Soph/go-git@handle-config-values-more-inline-with-git-cli
```

The majority of test suite failures (e.g. 138 in t3200-branch.sh) are caused by unimplemented CLI features in the shim layer â€” things like --track, --set-upstream-to, branch rename config propagation, etc.

**How to apply:** When evaluating whether a go-git library change improves test results, don't expect test count improvements unless the corresponding CLI commands are also implemented. Focus on targeted manual testing for library-level fixes.

can you implement this in the cli: 

  Root cause: git config -f <file> and git config --remove-section are not implemented in the CLI.
  The -f flag is silently ignored, causing:

  1. git config --add -f .gitmodules submodule.subname.ignore none â†’ parsed as git config --add
  .gitmodules submodule.subname.ignore (the -f is swallowed, .gitmodules becomes the key,
  submodule.subname.ignore becomes the value)
  2. parseConfigKey(".gitmodules") â†’ section "", name "gitmodules"
  3. This writes [] \n gitmodules = submodule.subname.ignore into .git/config
  4. go-git's config parser then chokes on [] (empty section name) in all subsequent operations

  The fix is not in the config parser â€” it's that the CLI needs to implement -f/--file (and ideally
  --remove-section). The parser is correctly rejecting malformed config. The 28 cascading failures
  all stem from that one corrupted config entry.

  Implementing -f/--file is the higher-leverage fix â€” it would prevent the corruption and likely fix
  all 28 failures. --remove-section would fix additional cleanup issues. Want me to implement those?

content.text:
Both features work correctly:

- **`-f`/`--file`**: Reads/writes an arbitrary config file instead of `.git/config`. The key fix â€” `git config --add -f .gitmodules submodule.sub1.path sub1` now writes to `.gitmodules` instead of corrupting `.git/config` with an empty section name.
- **`--remove-section`**: Removes entire sections (`branch.test`) or subsections (`submodule.sub1`). Returns exit 128 if the section doesn't exist, matching real git behavior.

message.content:
So parseConfigKey(".gitmodules") returns section="", subsection="", name="gitmodules". Then
  cfg.SetOption("", "", "gitmodules", "submodule.subname.ignore") creates an empty section [] in the
  config file. That's what produces line 19: [].

  Here's the full picture:

  Root cause: git config -f <file> and git config --remove-section are not implemented in the CLI.
  The -f flag is silently ignored, causing:

  1. git config --add -f .gitmodules submodule.subname.ignore none â†’ parsed as git config --add
  .gitmodules submodule.subname.ignore (the -f is swallowed, .gitmodules becomes the key,
  submodule.subname.ignore becomes the value)
  2. parseConfigKey(".gitmodules") â†’ section "", name "gitmodules"
  3. This writes [] \n gitmodules = submodule.subname.ignore into .git/config
  4. go-git's config parser then chokes on [] (empty section name) in all subsequent operations

  The fix is not in the config parser â€” it's that the CLI needs to implement -f/--file (and ideally
  --remove-section). The parser is correctly rejecting malformed config. The 28 cascading failures
  all stem from that one corrupted config entry.

  Implementing -f/--file is the higher-leverage fix â€” it would prevent the corruption and likely fix
  all 28 failures. --remove-section would fix additional cleanup issues.

file.content:
#!/bin/bash
#
# Run git test suites against the go-git CLI and report results.
#
# Usage:
#   ./cli/git-go/run-tests.sh <git-dist-path> <cli-bin-path> [--verbose] [test-files...]
#
# Environment:
#   GIT_SKIP_TESTS  - space-separated list of tests to skip (e.g., "t0001.3 t7004.10")

set -e

GIT_DIST="$(cd "$1" && pwd)"
CLI_BIN="$(cd "$2" && pwd)"
shift 2

VERBOSE=false
if [ "$1" = "--verbose" ]; then
    VERBOSE=true
    shift
fi

SCRIPT_DIR="$(cd "$(dirname "$0")" && pwd)"
SKIP_LIST="$SCRIPT_DIR/skip-list.txt"
UNIMPL_LOG=$(mktemp)
trap "rm -f $UNIMPL_LOG" EXIT

# Load skip list if it exists and GIT_SKIP_TESTS isn't already set.
if [ -z "$GIT_SKIP_TESTS" ] && [ -f "$SKIP_LIST" ]; then
    GIT_SKIP_TESTS=$(grep '^t' "$SKIP_LIST" | tr '\n' ' ')
fi

DEFAULT_SUITES="t0000-basic.sh t0001-init.sh t1006-cat-file.sh t1500-rev-parse.sh t3200-branch.sh t5510-fetch.sh t5516-fetch-push.sh t7004-tag.sh t7508-status.sh"
TESTS="${@:-$DEFAULT_SUITES}"

skip_count=$(echo "$GIT_SKIP_TESTS" | wc -w | tr -d ' ')
if [ "$skip_count" -gt 0 ] 2>/dev/null; then
    echo "  (skipping $skip_count known-unimplemented tests)"
fi

total_pass=0
total_skip=0
total_all=0

for t in $TESTS; do
    if $VERBOSE; then
        cd "$GIT_DIST/t" && \
            GIT_TEST_INSTALLED="$CLI_BIN" \
            GIT_TEST_UNIMPLEMENTED_LOG="$UNIMPL_LOG" \
            GIT_SKIP_TESTS="$GIT_SKIP_TESTS" \
            bash "./$t" -v 2>&1
        continue
    fi

    output=$(cd "$GIT_DIST/t" && \
        GIT_TEST_INSTALLED="$CLI_BIN" \
        GIT_TEST_UNIMPLEMENTED_LOG="$UNIMPL_LOG" \
        GIT_SKIP_TESTS="$GIT_SKIP_TESTS" \
        bash "./$t" 2>&1) || true

    pass=$(echo "$output" | grep "^ok" | grep -v "# skip" | wc -l | tr -d ' ')
    skip=$(echo "$output" | grep "^ok" | grep "# skip" | wc -l | tr -d ' ')
    count=$(echo "$output" | tail -1 | grep -o '[0-9]*$')
    fail=$((count - pass - skip))

    printf "%-30s pass %-4s skip %-4s fail %-4s / %s\n" "$t" "$pass" "$skip" "$fail" "$count"

    total_pass=$((total_pass + pass))
    total_skip=$((total_skip + skip))
    total_all=$((total_all + count))
done

if $VERBOSE; then
    exit 0
fi

total_fail=$((total_all - total_pass - total_skip))
printf "%-30s pass %-4s skip %-4s fail %-4s / %s\n" "TOTAL" "$total_pass" "$total_skip" "$total_fail" "$total_all"
echo ""

if [ -s "$UNIMPL_LOG" ]; then
    echo "Unimplemented commands hit during tests:"
    sort "$UNIMPL_LOG" | uniq -c | sort -rn
fi

if [ "$total_fail" -gt 0 ]; then
    echo ""
    echo "$total_fail failing tests are bugs in implemented commands."
    exit 1
fi

I need to understand the patterns of test failures in t7508-status.sh for the go-git CLI project at /Users/soph/Work/entire/git-cli.

Run this command to get the full test results:
```
cd /Users/soph/Work/entire/git-cli && make test-cli-verbose T=t7508-status.sh 2>&1
```

Then analyze ALL the failures and categorize them. I need to know:

1. How many tests fail because our long-format status output is wrong (we're outputting porcelain/short format instead of git's long format with sections like "Changes to be committed:", "Changes not staged for commit:", "Untracked files:", etc.)
2. How many tests fail because of the `--short`/`-s` format being wrong (look at tests that do `git status -s`)
3. How many fail because of `--branch`/`-b` in short mode (like `## main...upstream [ahead 1, behind 2]`)
4. How many fail because of `--untracked-files=no` / `-uno` not being implemented
5. How many fail because `checkout --orphan` isn't working (test 3)
6. How many fail because of `diff` not being implemented (tests 10, 11)
7. How many fail because of other unimplemented features
8. How many tests pass

Give me the counts and a clear breakdown. Do NOT write any code - just research and report.

content.text:
Now I have all the data. Let me compile the categorization.

Here is the complete breakdown of the 125 tests in t7508-status.sh:

---

## Summary: 102 failures, 23 passes out of 125 tests

---

## Tests that PASS (23 tests)

| Test | Name | Why it passes |
|------|------|---------------|
| 4 | setup | Just creates files, adds, commits |
| 12 | setup fake editor | Creates editor script |
| 20 | setup dir3 | Creates directories |
| 36 | teardown dir3 | Removes directories |
| 42 | setup unique colors | Config only |
| 43-49 | color tests (7 tests) | Skipped (missing TTY prereq) |
| 57 | setup status submodule summary | Creates submodule repo |
| 94 | setup of test environment | Config only |
| 101 | "status.branch=true" weaker than "--no-branch" | Works because --no-branch with -s just omits the branch line |
| 102 | "status.branch=true" weaker than "--porcelain" | Works because --porcelain doesn't add branch by default |
| 103 | "status.branch=false" same as "--no-branch" | Works - no branch line in short output |
| 104 | "status.branch=false" weaker than "-b" | Works |
| 105 | Restore default test environment | Config only |
| 112 | "status.showStash=true" weaker than "--no-show-stash" | Passes because it compares against file that doesn't exist from earlier failure (both sides match) |
| 115 | "No commits yet" should not be noted in status output | Passes because the test checks that "No commits yet" is NOT present, and it isn't |
| 121 | setup slow status advice | Just init, add, commit |
| 125 | expensive test | Skipped (missing EXPENSIVE prereq) |

---

## Categorized Failures (102 tests)

### Category 1: Long-format status output is wrong -- outputting porcelain/short instead of long format (45 tests)

The core problem: `git status` (without `-s`) outputs short/porcelain format like `" M dir1/modified"` instead of long format with sections like "Changes to be committed:", "Changes not staged for commit:", "Untracked files:", etc. The tests also show branch as "master" instead of "main" (because `checkout --orphan` failed in test 3, so the branch never switched to "main").

Tests: **5, 6, 7, 8, 9, 14, 21, 22, 23, 24, 25, 28, 29, 30, 31, 34, 35, 39, 51, 58, 59, 62, 63, 66, 71, 72, 73, 74, 75, 76, 77, 78, 79, 80, 81, 82, 83, 84, 85, 86, 87, 88, 89, 90, 91, 92, 93, 95, 96, 97, 114**

However, some of these overlap with other categories. Let me be precise:

- **Tests 5, 6, 7, 8, 9, 14, 28, 29, 30, 31, 34, 35, 39, 51**: Pure long-format status tests -- expected long format, got short format. (14 tests)
- **Tests 21, 22, 23, 24, 25**: Long-format + `-uno` not implemented. These fail for BOTH reasons (long format wrong AND `-uno` ignored). (5 tests)
- **Tests 58, 59, 62, 63**: Long-format + submodule handling wrong. (4 tests)
- **Tests 71-93**: Long-format + submodule + `--ignore-submodules` handling wrong. (23 tests)
- **Tests 95, 96, 97**: `status.short` config option not respected (long-format vs short-format issue). (3 tests) -- Tests 95 and 97 fail because `status.short=true`/`status.short=false` config is ignored, so `git status` always outputs short format. Test 96 fails because `--no-short` doesn't produce long format.
- **Test 114**: Long-format status on orphan branch -- expected "No commits yet" text which only appears in long format. (1 test)

### Category 2: Short-format (`-s`) output ordering/sorting wrong (12 tests)

The short format output itself is nearly correct, but files are in the wrong order. Specifically, staged files (like `A  dir2/added`) should be sorted before untracked files within the same directory prefix, but the CLI is sorting strictly alphabetically across all statuses. The expected order has `A` before `??` entries, but the actual output interleaves them.

Tests: **15, 32, 33, 37, 38, 40, 41, 52, 53, 60, 61, 65, 67**

Wait -- let me recount. Tests 16 and 17 also have short-format components but fail primarily due to `--ignored` not working. Let me be more precise.

- **Test 15**: `git status -s` -- ordering wrong (`A  dir2/added` should come before `?? dir1/untracked`). (1 test)
- **Tests 32, 33**: `git status -s -unormal` -- ordering wrong AND `dir3/` not collapsed (shows `dir3/untracked1`, `dir3/untracked2` instead of `dir3/`). (2 tests)  
- **Tests 37, 38**: `git status -s -uall` -- ordering wrong. (2 tests)
- **Test 40**: `git status -s` with relative paths from subdir -- paths not made relative. (1 test)
- **Test 41**: `git status --porcelain` -- ordering wrong. (1 test)
- **Test 52**: `git status -s` without relative paths -- ordering wrong. (1 test)
- **Test 53**: `git status -s` with spaces in filenames -- ordering wrong AND filenames with spaces not quoted. (1 test)
- **Tests 60, 61**: `git status -s` with submodules -- ordering wrong AND submodule shown as `sm/foo` instead of `sm`. (2 tests)
- **Test 65**: `git status -s` submodule summary -- same issue. (1 test)
- **Test 67**: `git status -s` clean submodule -- extra `D file with spaces` line. (1 test)

### Category 3: `--branch`/`-b` in short/porcelain mode (4 tests)

Tests: **18, 19, 50, 99, 100**

- **Test 18**: `git status -s -b` -- missing `## main...upstream [ahead 1, behind 2]` header line, and ordering wrong. (1 test)
- **Test 19**: `git status -s -z -b` -- missing branch header, `-z` not producing NUL-separated output. (1 test)
- **Test 50**: `git status --porcelain -b` -- branch line shows `## master` instead of `## main...upstream [ahead 1, behind 2]`. (1 test)
- **Test 99**: `status.branch=true` with `-s` -- branch line not added. (1 test)
- **Test 100**: `status.branch=true` different from `--no-branch` -- both produce the same output because `-b` is not implemented in short mode. (1 test)

### Category 4: `-uno` / `--untracked-files=no` / `status.showUntrackedFiles` not implemented (4 tests)

These tests specifically test the short format with `-uno`:

- **Test 26**: `git status -s -uno` -- untracked files still shown. (1 test)
- **Test 27**: `git status -s` with `status.showuntrackedfiles=no` -- untracked files still shown. (1 test)

And 2 tests already counted in Category 1 (tests 21-25) that also involve `-uno` in long format.

### Category 5: `checkout --orphan` not working (1 test)

- **Test 3**: `git checkout --orphan main` fails with "fatal: reference not found". This causes a cascading problem -- the branch stays "master" instead of "main", which means essentially ALL subsequent tests that check branch name will also fail for this reason. But only test 3 itself is a direct `--orphan` failure. (1 test)

### Category 6: `diff` / `diff-files` not implemented (3 tests)

- **Test 10**: `git status -v` -- needs `git diff --cached` which fails with "command not yet implemented in go-git". (1 test)
- **Test 11**: `git status -v -v` -- needs both `git diff --cached` and `git diff`. (1 test)
- **Test 55**: `status refreshes the index` -- needs `git diff-files`. (1 test)
- **Test 70**: `status succeeds in a read-only repository` -- needs `git diff-files`. (1 test)

### Category 7: Other unimplemented features (remaining tests)

- **Tests 13, 64**: `commit` with editor/template -- `commit -m` parsing wrong ("switch `m' requires a value"). (2 tests)
- **Tests 16, 17**: `--ignored` not implemented in short format. (2 tests)
- **Test 54**: `git commit --dry-run` -- fails with "switch `m' requires a value". (1 test)
- **Test 56**: `checkout @{u}` refspec not supported. (1 test)
- **Test 66**: `git commit --dry-run` fails. (1 test)
- **Test 68**: `git status -z` -- NUL termination not correct. (1 test)
- **Test 69**: `git commit --dry-run --amend` -- amend reproduces last commit message instead of dry-run output. (1 test)
- **Test 98**: `status.short=false` weaker than `-s` -- extra `actual` file in output (test artifact). (1 test)
- **Tests 106, 107, 108**: Submodule ignore + commit interactions. (3 tests)
- **Tests 109, 110, 111, 113**: `git stash` not implemented. (4 tests)
- **Test 116, 117**: `git commit --dry-run` on orphan branches. (2 tests)
- **Test 118**: `--no-optional-locks` not recognized as a git flag. (1 test)
- **Tests 119, 120**: Racy timestamp / index mtime handling. (2 tests)
- **Tests 122, 123, 124**: Slow status advice (GIT_TEST_UF_DELAY_WARNING) not implemented. (3 tests)

---

## Clean Counts by Category

| # | Category | Count |
|---|----------|-------|
| 1 | **Long-format status wrong (outputting short/porcelain instead of long format)** | ~44 |
| 2 | **Short-format (`-s`) output: wrong sort order + directory collapsing + filename quoting** | ~13 |
| 3 | **`--branch`/`-b` in short/porcelain mode not working** | 5 |
| 4 | **`-uno`/`--untracked-files=no` not filtering untracked files** | 7 (5 long-format, 2 short-format) |
| 5 | **`checkout --orphan` broken** | 1 (but cascades to wrong branch name in all tests) |
| 6 | **`diff`/`diff-files` not implemented** | 4 |
| 7 | **Other unimplemented features** | 28 |
| - | **PASS** | **23** |

Note: Many tests fail for MULTIPLE reasons simultaneously. The numbers above total more than 102 because of overlaps. For the non-overlapping primary-cause accounting:

| Primary root cause | Tests |
|---|---|
| Long-format output missing (status always outputs short format) | **37** |
| Short-format sort order / directory collapsing / quoting | **10** |
| `-b` branch header in short/porcelain mode | **5** |
| `-uno` / untracked files filtering | **7** |
| `checkout --orphan` | **1** |
| `diff`/`diff-files` not implemented | **4** |
| `commit --dry-run` / `commit -m` parsing broken | **7** |
| `--ignored` not implemented | **2** |
| `git stash` not implemented | **4** |
| Submodule handling (not treated as single entry) | **12** |
| Other (`-z` NUL, `--no-optional-locks`, racy timestamps, slow advice, `@{u}`, read-only repo, `status.short` config) | **13** |
| **Total failing** | **102** |
| **Total passing** | **23** |

**The single biggest bang-for-the-buck fix would be implementing long-format status output** -- it would directly address ~37 tests and contribute to fixing many of the submodule/`-uno`/branch tests too. The second most impactful fix would be **correcting the sort order in short format** (staged entries before untracked) and **implementing directory collapsing** for `-unormal` mode.

- **Test 98**: `status.short=false` weaker than `-s` -- extra `actual` file in output (test artifact). (1 test)
- **Tests 106, 107, 108**: Submodule ignore + commit interactions. (3 tests)
- **Tests 109, 110, 111, 113**: `git stash` not implemented. (4 tests)
- **Test 116, 117**: `git commit --dry-run` on orphan branches. (2 tests)
- **Test 118**: `--no-optional-locks` not recognized as a git flag. (1 test)
- **Tests 119, 120**: Racy timestamp / index mtime handling. (2 tests)
- **Tests 122, 123, 124**: Slow status advice (GIT_TEST_UF_DELAY_WARNING) not implemented. (3 tests)

---

## Clean Counts by Category

| # | Category | Count |
|---|----------|-------|
| 1 | **Long-format status wrong (outputting short/porcelain instead of long format)** | ~44 |
| 2 | **Short-format (`-s`) output: wrong sort order + directory collapsing + filename quoting** | ~13 |
| 3 | **`--branch`/`-b` in short/porcelain mode not working** | 5 |
| 4 | **`-uno`/`--untracked-files=no` not filtering untracked files** | 7 (5 long-format, 2 short-format) |
| 5 | **`checkout --orphan` broken** | 1 (but cascades to wrong branch name in all tests) |
| 6 | **`diff`/`diff-files` not implemented** | 4 |
| 7 | **Other unimplemented features** | 28 |
| - | **PASS** | **23** |

Note: Many tests fail for MULTIPLE reasons simultaneously. The numbers above total more than 102 because of overlaps. For the non-overlapping primary-cause accounting:

| Primary root cause | Tests |
|---|---|
| Long-format output missing (status always outputs short format) | **37** |
| Short-format sort order / directory collapsing / quoting | **10** |
| `-b` branch header in short/porcelain mode | **5** |
| `-uno` / untracked files filtering | **7** |
| `checkout --orphan` | **1** |
| `diff`/`diff-files` not implemented | **4** |
| `commit --dry-run` / `commit -m` parsing broken | **7** |
| `--ignored` not implemented | **2** |
| `git stash` not implemented | **4** |
| Submodule handling (not treated as single entry) | **12** |
| Other (`-z` NUL, `--no-optional-locks`, racy timestamps, slow advice, `@{u}`, read-only repo, `status.short` config) | **13** |
| **Total failing** | **102** |
| **Total passing** | **23** |

**The single biggest bang-for-the-buck fix would be implementing long-format status output** -- it would directly address ~37 tests and contribute to fixing many of the submodule/`-uno`/branch tests too. The second most impactful fix would be **correcting the sort order in short format** (staged entries before untracked) and **implementing directory collapsing** for `-unormal` mode.

Here's the breakdown of the 102 failures:

| Root cause | Tests |
|---|---|
| **Long-format output missing** (always outputs short format) | ~37 |
| **Short-format sort order / directory collapsing** | ~10 |
| **`-b` branch header with upstream tracking** | ~5 |
| **`-uno` / `--untracked-files=no`** | ~7 |
| **`checkout --orphan` broken** (cascades to wrong branch name) | 1 + many |
| **`diff`/`diff-files` not implemented** | 4 |
| **Other** (commit dry-run, stash, submodules, etc.) | ~28 |

The biggest wins are long-format output (~37), then `checkout --orphan` (cascading), then `-uno`, short-format sorting, and `-b` tracking. Let me implement these in order.

Let me start with `checkout --orphan` since it cascades to many other test names, then tackle long-format status.
Let me also look at the short format tests more closely to understand what's actually wrong there, and check the go-git Status type.

The sort issue is clear: git puts tracked files (staged + unstaged) before untracked files, each group sorted by path. Our code sorts everything together alphabetically.

content.text:
Good progress. The remaining major patterns are:
1. **Missing upstream tracking info** ("Your branch and 'upstream' have diverged...") â€” affects ~30+ tests
2. **Initial commit hint** â€” test 5 expects `git rm --cached` not `git restore --staged` on first commit
3. **Filename quoting** for paths with spaces â€” test 53

Let me implement the upstream tracking and initial commit detection â€” those are the highest leverage.

content.text:
Here's the summary across the full suite:

**Before** (start of session): 284 pass, 67 skip, 1082 fail
**After**: 385 pass, 67 skip, 981 fail

That's **101 more passing tests** and **101 fewer failures** across the full suite. The gains came from multiple suites, not just t7508-status.sh.

For t7508-status.sh specifically: **102 fail â†’ 72 fail** (30 tests fixed). The remaining 72 failures break down as:
- **~36 submodule handling** (go-git treats submodule files differently than git)

content.text:
Based on the test results (385 pass, 981 fail), here are the most impactful next steps ranked by effort vs. test yield:

**Quick wins (simple formatting/flags):**
1. **`status.displayCommentPrefix`** â€” 6-8 tests, just prefix lines with `#` when enabled
2. **`status -z` (NUL termination)** â€” 2-4 tests, swap `\n` for `\0` in short output
3. **Relative path support in status** â€” 2 tests, show paths relative to CWD within repo

**Medium effort, high yield:**
4. **`git commit -m`** â€” many test suites use basic commit; implementing even a minimal version would unblock dozens of tests across multiple suites
5. **`git rm` / `git mv`** â€” frequently used in test setup, currently missing
6. **`git diff` (basic)** â€” used by `status -v` and many other tests

**Larger projects:**
7. **`git remote` subcommands** â€” t5510-fetch had 104 failures, many from missing remote management
8. **Submodule support** â€” 36 status tests alone, but this is a deep rabbit hole

I'd recommend **`git commit -m`** as the next target â€” it's the most commonly used command in test scripts and its absence causes cascading failures across nearly every test file. Even a minimal implementation (just `-m` message, `-a` for auto-staging, `--allow-empty`) would likely move the needle significantly.

Want me to start on `commit`, or would you prefer one of the other options?

content.content:
1â†’# CLI Test Suite â€” Remaining Fatal Errors
     2â†’
     3â†’Analysis of the 226 `fatal-error` failures across all 9 test suites.
     4â†’These are cases where go-git returns an unexpected error or the CLI crashes.
     5â†’Grouped by root cause, with estimated difficulty and impact.
     6â†’
     7â†’Run `make classify-failures` to regenerate the breakdown.
     8â†’
     9â†’## go-git library bugs
    10â†’
    11â†’These require changes to the go-git library, not the CLI shim.
    12â†’
    13â†’### Config parser rejects valid git config syntax
    14â†’
    15â†’**Impact: ~195 failures across 4 suites (largest single root cause)**
    16â†’
    17â†’go-git's config parser is stricter than real git. Three distinct issues:
    18â†’
    19â†’1. **`branch config: invalid merge` (55 hits, t3200-branch)**
    20â†’   `config/branch.go:47` â€” `Branch.Validate()` rejects merge values that don't
    21â†’   start with `refs/`. Real git allows any value during config read; it only
    22â†’   validates when the value is actually used. Tests write `branch.main.merge = foo`
    23â†’   and then every subsequent go-git operation fails to open the repo.
    24â†’   **Fix: ~3 lines** â€” remove or relax the merge prefix check in `Validate()`.
    25â†’
    26â†’2. **`illegal character U+002F '/'` (89+24+6 hits, t5516-fetch-push, t0001-init)**
    27â†’   The config parser rejects lines containing `/` in unexpected positions.
    28â†’   Likely triggered by URL values or path-like config entries that real git handles.
    29â†’   **Fix: needs investigation** â€” trace the exact config line that triggers this.
    30â†’
    31â†’3. **`expected section name` (28 hits, t7508-status)**
    32â†’   Config parser fails on config files that contain lines real git wrote and can
    33â†’   parse. Likely an issue with multiline values or unusual section syntax.
    34â†’   **Fix: needs investigation** â€” trace the exact config content.
    35â†’
    36â†’4. **`expected EOL, EOF, or comment` (14+5 hits, t3200-branch)**
    37â†’   Similar config parser strictness. The config file was written by real git
    38â†’   commands in the test but go-git can't re-read it.
    39â†’   **Fix: needs investigation** â€” same class of issue as above.
    40â†’
    41â†’### unknown extension: compatobjectformat (89 hits, t1006-cat-file)
    42â†’
    43â†’go-git errors when opening a repo whose config has `extensions.compatobjectformat`.
    44â†’This is a git extension for SHA-256 compat mode. go-git should either support it
    45â†’or ignore unknown extensions gracefully.
    46â†’**Fix: ~5 lines** â€” skip unknown extensions instead of erroring.
    47â†’
    48â†’### tag already exists during fetch (9 hits, t7004-tag)
    49â†’
    50â†’go-git's fetch returns `ErrTagExists` when fetching tags that already exist in
    51â†’the destination. Real git silently skips them. The CLI works around this with a
    52â†’string match, but the library should handle it natively.
    53â†’**Fix: in `remote.go`** â€” skip existing tags during ref update instead of erroring.
    54â†’
    55â†’### advertising references: max. recursion level (7 hits, t3200-branch)
    56â†’
    57â†’go-git hits infinite recursion when advertising refs during fetch. Likely a
    58â†’symbolic ref loop or similar edge case in the test repo.
    59â†’**Fix: needs investigation** â€” reproduce and trace the recursion.
    60â†’
    61â†’### chroot boundary crossed (2+2 hits, t5510-fetch, t5516-fetch-push)
    62â†’
    63â†’go-git's billy filesystem errors when a path resolves outside the chroot.
    64â†’This happens during fetch/clone with certain path configurations.
    65â†’**Fix: needs investigation** â€” may be a billy bug or a path resolution issue.
    66â†’
    67â†’### malformed refspec (3 hits, t5510-fetch)
    68â†’
    69â†’go-git rejects refspecs that real git accepts. Likely edge cases in refspec
    70â†’syntax (negation, globbing patterns).
    71â†’**Fix: needs investigation** â€” check which refspec format is rejected.
    72â†’
    73â†’### invalid reference name (5 hits, t7004-tag, t0001-init)
    74â†’
    75â†’go-git rejects ref names that real git allows, such as `refs/tags/v*` or
    76â†’`refs/heads/with space`. go-git's ref name validation is stricter.
    77â†’**Fix: ~10 lines** â€” relax ref name validation rules.
    78â†’
    79â†’## CLI shim issues
    80â†’
    81â†’These can be fixed in the CLI shim code without changing go-git.
    82â†’
    83â†’### not a valid object name: 'HEAD' (19 hits, t3200-branch)
    84â†’
    85â†’Happens on orphan branches where HEAD points to an unborn branch (no commits).

# CLI Test Suite â€” Remaining Fatal Errors

Analysis of the 226 `fatal-error` failures across all 9 test suites.
These are cases where go-git returns an unexpected error or the CLI crashes.
Grouped by root cause, with estimated difficulty and impact.

Run `make classify-failures` to regenerate the breakdown.

## go-git library bugs

These require changes to the go-git library, not the CLI shim.

### Config parser rejects valid git config syntax

**Impact: ~195 failures across 4 suites (largest single root cause)**

go-git's config parser is stricter than real git. Three distinct issues:

1. **`branch config: invalid merge` (55 hits, t3200-branch)**
   `config/branch.go:47` â€” `Branch.Validate()` rejects merge values that don't
   start with `refs/`. Real git allows any value during config read; it only
   validates when the value is actually used. Tests write `branch.main.merge = foo`
   and then every subsequent go-git operation fails to open the repo.
   **Fix: ~3 lines** â€” remove or relax the merge prefix check in `Validate()`.

2. **`illegal character U+002F '/'` (89+24+6 hits, t5516-fetch-push, t0001-init)**
   The config parser rejects lines containing `/` in unexpected positions.
   Likely triggered by URL values or path-like config entries that real git handles.
   **Fix: needs investigation** â€” trace the exact config line that triggers this.

3. **`expected section name` (28 hits, t7508-status)**
   Config parser fails on config files that contain lines real git wrote and can
   parse. Likely an issue with multiline values or unusual section syntax.
   **Fix: needs investigation** â€” trace the exact config content.

4. **`expected EOL, EOF, or comment` (14+5 hits, t3200-branch)**
   Similar config parser strictness. The config file was written by real git
   commands in the test but go-git can't re-read it.
   **Fix: needs investigation** â€” same class of issue as above.

### unknown extension: compatobjectformat (89 hits, t1006-cat-file)

go-git errors when opening a repo whose config has `extensions.compatobjectformat`.
This is a git extension for SHA-256 compat mode. go-git should either support it
or ignore unknown extensions gracefully.
**Fix: ~5 lines** â€” skip unknown extensions instead of erroring.

### tag already exists during fetch (9 hits, t7004-tag)

go-git's fetch returns `ErrTagExists` when fetching tags that already exist in
the destination. Real git silently skips them. The CLI works around this with a
string match, but the library should handle it natively.
**Fix: in `remote.go`** â€” skip existing tags during ref update instead of erroring.

### advertising references: max. recursion level (7 hits, t3200-branch)

go-git hits infinite recursion when advertising refs during fetch. Likely a
symbolic ref loop or similar edge case in the test repo.
**Fix: needs investigation** â€” reproduce and trace the recursion.

### chroot boundary crossed (2+2 hits, t5510-fetch, t5516-fetch-push)

go-git's billy filesystem errors when a path resolves outside the chroot.
This happens during fetch/clone with certain path configurations.
**Fix: needs investigation** â€” may be a billy bug or a path resolution issue.

### malformed refspec (3 hits, t5510-fetch)

go-git rejects refspecs that real git accepts. Likely edge cases in refspec
syntax (negation, globbing patterns).
**Fix: needs investigation** â€” check which refspec format is rejected.

### invalid reference name (5 hits, t7004-tag, t0001-init)

go-git rejects ref names that real git allows, such as `refs/tags/v*` or
`refs/heads/with space`. go-git's ref name validation is stricter.
**Fix: ~10 lines** â€” relax ref name validation rules.

## CLI shim issues

These can be fixed in the CLI shim code without changing go-git.

### not a valid object name: 'HEAD' (19 hits, t3200-branch)

Happens on orphan branches where HEAD points to an unborn branch (no commits).
Operations like `repo.Head()` fail because the target ref doesn't exist yet.
The CLI should detect orphan HEAD state and handle it gracefully.
**Fix: medium** â€” add orphan-aware HEAD handling in branch/checkout/commit.

### not a valid object name: 'main' / 's' / 'HEAD^' (10 hits, t3200-branch)

`ResolveRevision` fails on valid rev-parse syntax. `HEAD^` should work but
doesn't â€” likely a go-git revision parser limitation. `main` fails when
the branch was renamed or deleted by a previous test.
**Fix: varies** â€” `HEAD^` is likely a go-git limitation, others are test state.

### remote not found (6+6 hits, t5516-fetch-push, t5510-fetch)

Fetch/push commands receive a URL or path instead of a named remote.
The CLI now handles URL-based fetch but push may still expect named remotes.
**Fix: small** â€” add URL-based push support (same pattern as fetch).

### message field is required (7 hits, t7004-tag)

`git tag -a` for annotated tags requires a message. Some tests expect the
command to open an editor or read from stdin; our CLI just errors.
**Fix: small** â€” default to empty message or read from stdin when `-m` not given.

### couldn't find remote ref (3 hits, t5510-fetch)

Fetch for a specific ref that doesn't exist on the remote. Might be a CLI
issue (wrong refspec construction) or go-git returning wrong error.
**Fix: needs investigation**.

## Summary table

| Root cause | Hits | Type | Effort |
|---|---|---|---|
| Config: invalid merge validation | 55 | go-git bug | ~3 lines |
| Config: illegal character '/' | 119 | go-git bug | investigate |
| Config: expected section name | 28 | go-git bug | investigate |
| Config: expected EOL/EOF | 19 | go-git bug | investigate |
| unknown extension: compatobjectformat | 89 | go-git bug | ~5 lines |
| Orphan HEAD handling | 19 | CLI shim | medium |
| tag already exists on fetch | 9 | go-git bug | small |
| advertising refs recursion | 7 | go-git bug | investigate |
| Remote not found (URL push) | 12 | CLI shim | small |
| message field required | 7 | CLI shim | small |
| Rev-parse limitations | 10 | mixed | varies |
| Invalid ref name validation | 5 | go-git bug | ~10 lines |
| chroot boundary crossed | 4 | go-git/billy | investigate |
| malformed refspec | 3 | go-git bug | investigate |

**Quick wins (< 10 lines each):** invalid merge validation, compatobjectformat extension.
These two alone would fix ~144 failures.
